footnote: Titles follow the historical albo d'oro back to 1968 — the pre-FIGC federations included. Honours follow legal continuity: sporting-title purchases don't transfer them, so today's Milan and Lazio carry none of the historic Milan- and Lazio-named crowns. The 12 clubs shown are the 2025–26 season's until newcomers Como 1907 debut in the autumn.

teams.name: FC Como Women
teams.firstGameNote: Conceded a first-half Girelli hat-trick in an 0–6 home debut against Juventus in 2022; the lineage, born Como 2000 in 1997, first reached Serie A back in 2001 and has never cracked the top half.

lastTitle.clinch: beat Tavagnacco 2–0 before 9,000 at the Franchi to clinch with a matchday to spare — the title carried into ACF Fiorentina when the women's club was absorbed in 2020

teams.name: Genoa
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 2–1 to Milan at La Sciorba in their 2025 debut after climbing from the regional leagues in six seasons — then went straight back down, the drop sealed in May 2026.

teams.name: Inter
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 2–2 with Hellas Verona in their 2019 debut, Julie Debever scoring the club's first top-flight goal; back-to-back runner-up finishes since — both times watching someone else lift it.

teams.name: Juventus

lastTitle.clinch: beat Milan 2–0 in Biella — a Cristiana Girelli brace — to wrap up scudetto number six early in the poule scudetto

teams.lineage: Founded 2017; champions in each of their first five seasons. The 2020 title was awarded by federal vote after COVID curtailed the season.

teams.name: Lazio
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 1–2 to Sampdoria at Formello in the modern section's 2021 top-flight debut under Carolina Morace; the five historic Lazio scudetti, last in 2002, remain with the predecessor club whose sporting title was acquired in 2015.

teams.name: AC Milan
teams.firstGameNote: Won 6–0 at Pink Bari in their 2018 debut — but the five Milan-named scudetti in the record books belong to unrelated historic clubs, and the 2018 club has since lost a league, a cup and a supercup final apiece.

teams.name: Napoli Women
teams.firstGameNote: Drew 1–1 with Grifo Perugia at the Collana in their 2012 top-flight debut as Carpisa Yamamay Napoli; three Serie B titles and several rebrands later, the ceiling remains mid-table.

teams.name: Parma
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 4–1 at Inter in their 2022 debut, weeks after buying Empoli's place in the league; relegated in year one, back by 2025, and safe in 2026 only once Genoa's relegation was sealed.

lastTitle.clinch: beat Ternana 2–0 at the Tre Fontane — both goals Manuela Giugliano, one a retaken penalty — to seal a third scudetto with two matchdays to spare

teams.name: Sassuolo
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 0–4 to Tavagnacco at the Mirabello in their 2017 debut and dropped their first seven matches; third place in 2021 is the club record, under president Elisabetta Vignotto, a legend of the Azzurre.

teams.name: Ternana
teams.firstGameNote: Lost 5–0 at Inter in their 2025 debut — the first Umbrian women's club ever to reach Serie A — then comfortably stayed up in year one.
